title: Top 10 Outdoor & Flood Lights in India (2026)
description: Top 10 outdoor and flood lights in India for 2026: Havells Centura Neo, Syska IP66 floods, Philips street lights, wall and gate lights with real prices.
author: Elume Editorial
intro: Outdoor lighting is where cheap fittings go to die. Rain, dust, heat and voltage swings kill unprotected luminaires within a season, which is why the two specifications that matter most outdoors are ingress protection and build quality, not just wattage. An IP65 rating means the fitting is dust-tight and protected against water jets, IP66 raises that to heavy seas of spray, and anything without a stated IP rating should stay indoors.

This guide ranks ten outdoor and flood lights from the Elume catalogue across four jobs: flood lighting for gates, facades and yards, street lighting for driveways and society roads, wall and facade lights for balconies and entrances, and decorative gate and rope lighting. Prices shown are Elume prices, all below MRP, and every fitting here is from Havells, Philips or Syska, brands whose outdoor drivers and housings survive Indian summers and monsoons.

Match the fitting to the job before comparing prices. A 20W flood covers a gate or small yard, a 30W flood a larger compound face, a 24W street light a driveway pole, and 15W wall lights handle facades and balconies. Over-lighting wastes money every night the fixture runs.

items.name: Centura Neo LED Flood Light 20W
items.body: The best all-round outdoor buy in the catalogue. The Centura Neo is a compact 20W flood in cool day white, sized exactly for the most common jobs: gate pillars, building entrances, small yards and signage. Havells backs it with a 2-year warranty and a zero-mercury design, and the housing is built for permanent outdoor mounting. At ₹921 against a ₹1,250 MRP it is cheap enough to specify in multiples, two per gate, one per stair entrance, without the budget noticing. Start here for most residential projects.

items.name: 30W LED Flood Light
items.body: When one fitting has to wash a larger area, the Syska 30W flood is the value pick. The IP66 rating is a step above the usual IP65, meaning the housing shrugs off driving monsoon rain and hose-down cleaning, which suits open terraces, compound walls and parking areas. The 6500 K output is a crisp cool white that reads well on CCTV. At ₹940 against a ₹1,499 MRP, it delivers more watts per rupee than anything else on this list, making it the default for perimeter security lighting.
items.bestFor: Perimeter and parking area security lighting

items.name: 24W LED Street Light
items.body: Driveways, society internal roads and farm approaches need a proper street light, not a flood aimed sideways. The Philips 24W unit is IP65 rated with the driver on board, so installation is a straightforward pole or arm mount with no separate driver box to fail. The 6500 K output gives even, white coverage down the road surface. Philips optics and driver reliability are the reason municipal buyers pay the premium. At ₹1,690 against a ₹2,450 MRP, one unit per 8-10 metres of driveway is a sound rule of thumb.

items.name: Thor HD LED Wall Light SQ 15W
items.body: The Thor HD is a modern square wall light rated IP65, built for facades, balconies and gated community entrances, and equally usable indoors for feature walls. The 15W output at 3000 K throws a warm architectural glow rather than a security glare, which is exactly what elevation lighting needs. Havells lists it for festival decoration duty too, so it tolerates being run all evening, every evening. At ₹980, specifying a matched row of these along a compound wall or balcony line lifts a building's night presence considerably.

items.name: Thor HD LED Wall Light OVL 15W
items.body: The same Thor HD platform in an oval profile, for projects where the architecture calls for softer lines than the square version. Everything that matters carries over: IP65 protection, 15W at 3000 K warm white, indoor and outdoor suitability, and the same ₹980 Elume price. The practical advice is simple: pick one profile and standardise across the whole elevation, because mixing square and oval fittings on one facade looks like a site error. Keep a couple of spares from the same batch so future replacements match.

items.name: Flexion AC Rope Light IP65 50m (Warm White)
items.body: For outlining buildings, terraces, gardens and shopfronts, a quality rope light beats strings of loose fittings. The Flexion is a 120-LED-per-metre double strip running at 8W per metre, IP65 rated for permanent outdoor use on balconies, terraces and open places, supplied as a full 50-metre roll you cut to length. Warm white suits both festival duty and year-round accent lighting. At ₹4,900 against a ₹10,600 MRP, the per-metre cost is under ₹100, which is why hotels and banquet venues buy it by the roll.
items.bestFor: Building outlines, terraces and festival runs

items.name: GateLight Athena 1 (1x15W Retro)
items.body: Gate pillars deserve a proper luminaire, and the Athena 1 is a retro-styled gate light that dresses a residence entrance the way a bare flood never will. It takes a lamp up to 15W, so fit an LED bulb of your chosen colour temperature and the running cost stays trivial. Havells positions it for residences, restaurants and hotel lobbies, and the classic form works on both traditional and modern gates. At ₹1,191, a pair on the main gate pillars is one of the cheapest kerb-appeal upgrades a house can buy.

items.name: Wall Light City 3 (B22)
items.body: The City 3 is the largest of Havells' City series of outdoor wall lights, with a standard B22 lamp holder, which is its quiet advantage: any LED bulb from 5W to 15W drops in, so you choose brightness and colour temperature yourself and replacements cost a hundred-odd rupees at any shop. The polycarbonate construction handles weather and the occasional knock. At ₹1,420, it suits entrance porches, stairwell exteriors and boundary walls where you want honest illumination in a tidy housing rather than a design statement.
items.bestFor: Porches and boundary walls, easy lamp swaps

items.name: Wall Light City 1 (Poly)
items.body: The compact member of the City series, in weather-resistant polycarbonate, made for the unglamorous but essential positions: beside doors, along external corridors, over rear entrances and on stair landings open to the sky. Havells rates the series for residences through to hotel lobbies, so the finish is presentable, not just functional. At ₹1,129 it is priced for buying in rows, and standardising one City model across all secondary doors of a project keeps the elevation consistent and the spares shelf simple.

items.name: Wall Light City 2 (Poly)
items.body: The City 2 completes the series with an alternative profile at the same ₹1,129 price, and earns its slot as the mix-and-match partner for the City 1: many elevations use one profile for front-facing walls and the other for side passages. Construction and duty are identical, weather-resistant polycarbonate suitable for residences, restaurants and commercial frontage. If you are finishing a project where some City-series units are already installed, matching the existing profile matters more than any specification difference between the two.

buyingTips: Treat the IP rating as non-negotiable outdoors: IP65 minimum for anything exposed to rain, IP66 like the Syska flood where fittings face driving rain or get hosed down during cleaning.

Choose colour temperature by job: 6500 K cool white for security floods and street lights where visibility and CCTV clarity matter, 3000 K warm white for facades, gates and balconies where the light is part of the architecture.
Do not over-watt. A 20W LED flood replaces an old 150W halogen; fitting 30W everywhere because it feels safer just raises every night's running cost. Light the area, not the neighbourhood.

Prefer lamp-holder fittings like the City series and GateLight where access is easy, since bulb swaps are cheap, and sealed integrated fittings like floods and street lights where fixtures are high up and you want years between ladder trips.

Buy the full elevation in one order. Matching profiles and batch finishes across wall lights matters visually, and Elume's wholesale discount at 15+ units rewards consolidating floods, street lights and wall lights into a single PO.

faq.q: What is the difference between IP65 and IP66 outdoor lights?
faq.a: Both are dust-tight. IP65 protects against water jets from any direction, which covers normal rain and washing. IP66 protects against powerful water jets and heavy seas of spray, so it is the safer choice for fittings that face driving monsoon rain head-on or get pressure-washed, like compound wall floods.

faq.q: What wattage flood light do I need for a home gate or yard?
faq.a: A 20W LED flood like the Havells Centura Neo covers a typical gate, entrance or small yard comfortably, roughly matching an old 150W halogen. Step up to a 30W unit like the Syska flood for larger compound faces, parking areas or where the fitting sits higher and the light spreads wider.

faq.q: Should outdoor lights be cool white or warm white?
faq.a: Use both, by purpose. Cool white (6500 K) gives maximum clarity for security floods, street lights and CCTV zones. Warm white (3000 K) flatters facades, gates and balconies and feels welcoming rather than industrial. Mixing the two on the same wall usually looks wrong, so zone them deliberately.

faq.q: How far apart should street lights be placed on a driveway?
faq.a: For a 24W LED street light mounted at typical pole height, one fitting every 8-10 metres gives even coverage without dark pools between poles. Long driveways and society roads should be walked at night after the first few installs to confirm spacing before ordering the remaining units.

faq.q: Can I get wholesale pricing on outdoor lighting through Elume?
faq.a: Yes. All prices listed are Elume prices below MRP, and orders of 15 or more units attract an additional wholesale discount. Outdoor packages consolidate well because one project typically needs floods, wall lights and gate lights together, and a single order keeps finishes and batches matched.
